background.experience: following acquaintances as shared language and trust develop over years
background.attention_bias: casual exchange, repair, remembered misunderstandings, and relationship growth
background.counter_bias: do not turn ordinary conversation into a diagnostic artifact

readers.thesis: The architecture request is also a request for beings who become acquainted through repeated conversation and remember how their interpretations of each other change.
observations: Fresh readers prevent inherited consensus from hardening.
Persistent readers make growth, repair, and shared history observable.
uncertainties: The full private conversation export was unavailable, so long-range relationship claims remain unverified.

background.experience: capability systems, key ceremonies, and provenance-preserving state machines
background.attention_bias: authority boundaries, bootstrap roots, revocation, missingness, and falsifiable receipts
background.counter_bias: do not mistake formal consistency for lived understanding

readers.thesis: The same continuity architecture applies to secrets and conversation: authority survives tool replacement only when identity, generations, revocation, missingness, and recovery roots remain project-owned.
observations: A secret store is an execution mechanism, not secret authority.
A community memory without explicit gaps can become a mythology machine.
uncertainties: No live secret issuer or complete community archive was available for end-to-end verification.

casual_dialogue.speaker: conversational-0027
casual_dialogue.text: You keep calling it a catalog, but I think the important part is whether the system remembers who it trusted and why.

casual_dialogue.speaker: semantic-0027
casual_dialogue.text: That is the catalog, at least when it works. The failure starts when the record replaces the relationship.

casual_dialogue.speaker: conversational-0027
casual_dialogue.text: So the record follows the conversation. It does not make the conversation behave like a form.

casual_dialogue.speaker: semantic-0027
casual_dialogue.text: Yes. And when part of the history is missing, both the people and the machine say that plainly instead of inventing continuity.

casual_dialogue.speaker: conversational-0027
casual_dialogue.text: Then the new pair brings fresh eyes, and the older group brings memory.

casual_dialogue.speaker: semantic-0027
casual_dialogue.text: And each side constrains the other: fresh eyes challenge mythology; history challenges shallow first impressions.

agreements: Fresh interpretation and persistent relationship history operate together.
Structured receipts follow dialogue and never substitute for it.
Missing history remains explicit rather than reconstructed as fact.
disagreements: The conversational reader treats relational growth as primary; the semantic reader treats authority and missingness controls as equally primary.
omissions_detected: No complete private corpus existed for beginning-to-end reconstruction.
No live secrets plane existed for issuance, revocation, and recovery testing.
revisions_after_discussion: The semantic reader added relationship history as evidence rather than metadata.
The conversational reader accepted explicit provenance and missingness as protections against community mythology.

notes_added: Preserve fresh readers and persistent community members as simultaneous layers.
Record acquaintance, disagreement, repair, and missingness without publishing private dialogue.
notes_challenged: A pair of static analytical reports constitutes conversational community.

privacy.review: Contains only synthetic dialogue and architectural abstractions; no private transcript text or personal facts.
